scene: scene_1 — Maya turns her finished study into a call for a firm
detail: A 5-second glance returns a wall of undifferentiated 'Draft' cards — two literally named 'Attakar / Attakar' — so the hero 'finished plan -> one-click solicitation' story is not findable; the opening fails to establish the problem.
fix_recommendation: Lead the portfolio with the one finished R6 study in a distinct 'ready-to-solicit' lane with a one-line problem framing, so the scene's purpose reads in 5 seconds.

scene: scene_1 — Maya turns her finished study into a call for a firm
detail: 'Create solicitation' has zero primacy — it is the 3rd of four equal-weight text actions (Archive * Submit for review * Create solicitation * Delete) on every card, visually identical to Delete; the LIVE lane is an empty dead end and R1-R4 cards are near-empty.
fix_recommendation: Promote 'Create solicitation' to a single styled primary button and demote Archive/Submit/Delete into a '...' overflow; surface it primarily on the finished study.

scene: scene_1 — Maya turns her finished study into a call for a firm
detail: Fixture noise: duplicate 'Attakar / Attakar' cards, '1 wards' (broken pluralization that contradicts the page's own intervention+control study definition), a ragged grid of full-vs-empty cards, and faint low-contrast area/status metadata.
fix_recommendation: Suppress/de-dupe the seed-noise cards, fix singular/plural ('1 ward'), normalize card density, and raise contrast on area-count + status metadata. Product/seed-data fix, never a camera crop.

scene: scene_1 — Maya turns her finished study into a call for a firm
detail: Spine S3 rests on 'the approved plan' / 'a FINISHED micro-plan', but every visible plan card reads 'Draft' and the R6 study reads 'defining * 1 wards' — the finished-plan precondition the concept_claim depends on is contradicted by the still.
fix_recommendation: Re-seed/re-capture this scene with the R6 study in an Approved/Reviewed/finished state (and a ward count satisfying the intervention+control definition) so the 'launch from a finished plan' claim is visible, not just the button.

scene: scene_1 — Maya turns her finished study into a call for a firm
detail: Strong seed-data smell in a single still — duplicate-named cards, '1 wards', empty R1-R4 cards, and an empty 'LIVE ... none' lane read as a half-populated fixture rather than a clean capture.
fix_recommendation: Seed a realistic, de-duplicated portfolio with at least one finished study (and suppress empty placeholder lanes) before capture.

scene: scene_1 — Maya turns her finished study into a call for a firm
detail: ADVISORY (non-blocking). The 'launch from a finished plan' narration is not demonstrated — the Create-solicitation button sits on Draft cards and a 'defining / 1 wards' study; '1 ward' also contradicts the page's own intervention+control definition.
fix_recommendation: Show the button on a genuinely finished study whose ward count satisfies the intervention+control definition.

scene: scene_2 — Maya finds the plan already waiting as the coverage area
detail: What+why is legible (create form pre-attaches snapshotted coverage) but NO concrete user/role is named on screen — no owner identity/persona label — which caps concept_clarity at 3.
fix_recommendation: Name the planner on screen (an owner avatar/byline, e.g. 'Maya — Program Planner') so the role anchors the 'why'.

scene: scene_2 — Maya finds the plan already waiting as the coverage area
detail: The Coverage-areas panel is a coherent, clearly-attached affordance (green 'Attached' badge, 'Snapshotted at creation' note, 2 wards / 840 work areas / study arms), but the surrounding form is a sea of empty required-looking fields (deadline, contact email) with no required/optional distinction, and two empty sections (Questions, Criteria) give no preview.
fix_recommendation: Pre-fill or visibly mark required fields and de-emphasize optional ones — or capture one step later with key fields filled so the form reads as a confirm-and-publish moment, not a blank mid-fill.

scene: scene_2 — Maya finds the plan already waiting as the coverage area
detail: Clean type hierarchy and consistent controls, but the middle third is dominated by empty placeholder inputs (whitespace reads as 'unfinished'), and the coverage card's mini-map is a generic pin-in-a-square glyph rather than an actual map of the 2 wards / 840 work areas it claims.
fix_recommendation: Replace the coverage card's generic pin glyph with a real mini-map of the wards, and reduce the empty-placeholder dominance by pre-filling/marking required fields.

scene: scene_2 — Maya finds the plan already waiting as the coverage area
detail: Clean static frame with no render artifacts, but the all-empty mid-fill form state reads as mid-interaction rather than a settled confirm step; cursor parked oddly in the breadcrumb whitespace.
fix_recommendation: Capture the frame in a more settled (partly-filled) state and park the cursor off the breadcrumb text.

scene: scene_3 — Amina reads the open call and ticks the area she can staff
detail: The 'Which areas can you cover? — Select one or more' prompt renders exactly ONE checkbox (a degenerate, near-contradictory affordance), and the AI-coach card promises to review 'your draft answers against the evaluation criteria' for which no answer fields exist on the page — a capability/UI mismatch.
fix_recommendation: Relabel the single-area case honestly (or surface the real sub-areas so 'one or more' is true), and scope the AI-coach copy to what the page actually contains (the coverage selection), not 'draft answers'.

scene: scene_3 — Amina reads the open call and ticks the area she can staff
detail: Two saturated filled CTAs ('Get Feedback on My Answers' and 'Submit Response') collide for primary status, and ~40% of the frame is dead whitespace with the form floating in the upper third — a balance/hierarchy failure a careful viewer catches immediately.
fix_recommendation: Demote 'Get Feedback on My Answers' to a secondary/outline button and constrain/center the form card to remove the dead lower whitespace. Product change, never a camera crop.

scene: scene_3 — Amina reads the open call and ticks the area she can staff
detail: States what+why with a concrete role (survey firm) and clear problem (apply by picking coverage), but the single-option selector and the AI card referencing nonexistent 'answers' blur the one-sentence read.
fix_recommendation: Give the coverage option a distinct, self-describing label (geography + scope) so the choice is legibly a coverage AREA, not an echo of the solicitation title.

scene: scene_3 — Amina reads the open call and ticks the area she can staff
detail: ADVISORY (non-blocking). The AI-coach card claims to review 'your draft answers against the evaluation criteria', but the page exposes no answer fields/questions — only a single coverage checkbox; nothing for the coach to review as captured. The 'blocked until chosen' error string is correctly not visible (fires on submit) and was not deducted.
fix_recommendation: Make the coach copy reference what's actually on the page (the coverage selection), or add the answer fields the coach implies.

scene: scene_4 — Maya sees the firm tied to the coverage she designed
detail: ACTIONS header is complete and all three actions (View / Review / Award trophy) are fully in-frame (iter-2 clipping RESOLVED), but green is over-used across the coverage pill, the SELECTED-COVERAGE check, the 'Active' status dot, AND the Review icon — four greens with four unrelated meanings flatten the signal.
fix_recommendation: Reserve green for a single meaning (status). Make the coverage pill a calm neutral/indigo and use one consistent action accent with the primary highlighted.

scene: scene_5 — Maya looks ahead to standing up the real opportunity
detail: The beat must ADD the look-ahead/honesty about provisioning-as-next-step, but the frame is a near-duplicate of scene 4's responses table (byte-identical capture) with NO distinct on-screen affordance for the Award boundary — the 5-second read is 'same responses screen', a 'nothing new happened' result.
fix_recommendation: Either re-point scene 5 to a genuinely distinct capture (the Award control focused/hovered with an aspirational 'provision a live opportunity — next step' treatment) or give the final beat a distinct on-screen affordance. Surface to the user; do not auto-apply.

scene: scene_5 — Maya looks ahead to standing up the real opportunity
detail: The sequence does not visibly ADVANCE — this final beat is near-identical to (byte-identical capture of) the immediately prior responses scene with no distinct affordance, hover, focus, or aspirational treatment on the Award control, so it reads static.
fix_recommendation: Differentiate the final frame from scene 4 with a state change on Award (focus/hover/annotation) so the viewer perceives a deliberate forward step into the look-ahead. Surface to the user; do not auto-apply.

scene: scene_5 — Maya looks ahead to standing up the real opportunity
detail: ADVISORY (non-blocking). The 'Award as next-step boundary' look-ahead is narrated but NOT visible/distinct in the still — the Award control is identical to two fully-working actions with no boundary treatment. The honest deferral of opportunity-provisioning (G1) is correct behavior and was NOT penalized.
fix_recommendation: Optionally surface the deferral in-frame (a small 'provisioning a live opportunity — not yet built' note near Award) so the still carries the S7 provenance rather than only the voiceover.
